item.title: Retrieving values from Kotlin enum classes
item.description: Not long ago, when I was working on an Android project, I’ve noticed a problem with a Kotlin function that was using an enum. I needed to get a value from an enum class but because the data was received from an external API I needed to map it based on a value. So the enum class looked something like this: enum class WeirdEnum(val value: Int) { Zero, One, Two, Seven, Eight, Nine, Ten, Eleven, companion object { private val values = WeirdEnum.

item.title: My First Post
item.description: Hi My name is rogowskibart and I love making mobile and web apps. For the past 4 years I’ve been learning Java during my studies. I’ve worked as a Java developer for about 2 years. This year, however, I fell in love with Kotlin and I’m thrilled to learn as much about it as I can. I’ve always dreamed of teaching other people to code and make their own apps. I want to get better at teaching and sharing my knowledge.
If you feel that I brought you some value, please consider supporting me on one of these platforms. Become a Patron!
At this point, nothing interesting is happening here. As the time goes by, however, this page will grow.
